page: Microsoft Azure F8-4amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famdsv7 family. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a 945 GB disk, and the server is premium storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU test, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, falls into the average tier. Memory bandwidth is moderate, with cached small-block reads reaching 803.46 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads and writes at 52.32 GB/sec and 34.33 GB/sec respectively. For language model workloads, text generation with a small 135M model is top-tier at 331.4 tokens/sec, whereas prompt processing with a medium 7B model is weak at 29.74 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the F8-4amds_v7 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio alongside dedicated high-frequency AMD EPYC cores, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads without requiring excessive vCPU counts. However, a key tradeoff is the moderate multi-core scaling and weak prompt processing speeds on larger models, indicating it is less suited for highly parallelized compute-heavy tasks or large-scale AI inference. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers like Redis, and single-threaded data compression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F8-4amds_v7 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server from the Famdsv7 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor clocked at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core, and includes 945 GB of local storage. The server exhibits top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and small-model text generation. However, multi-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, and medium-model prompt processing is weak. Qualitatively, the server offers strong resource density for memory-intensive workloads without requiring high vCPU counts. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-bound applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8-4amds_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 64 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8-4amds_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-dense virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and generous local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs clocked at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM providing 16.0 GB per core
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for database operations, caching, and memory-bound tasks
